sections.heading: Step 1: Click .
sections.body:
- Select the collection.

- In the query bar, click the **Export Code** icon to open the query
  export card.

![Query bar export dropdown menu](/atlas-ui/images/atlas-ui/querybar/export-query-to-language-menu-option.png)

sections.heading: Step 2: Select your export language.
sections.body:
On the right side of the card, click the drop-down menu under
**Exported Query** and select your desired programming language.

The **My Query** pane on the left of the export card displays your
pipeline in [mongosh](mongodb-shell) syntax. The **Exported Query** pane
to the right displays your pipeline in the selected programming language.

![Query bar language select](/atlas-ui/images/atlas-ui/querybar/export-query-to-language-select.png)

sections.heading: Step 3:  Include import statements.
sections.body:
Click the **Include Import Statements** checkbox to include the
required import statements for the selected programming language.

sections.heading: Step 4:  Include driver syntax.
sections.body:
Click the **Include Driver Syntax** checkbox to include
application code for the selected programming language. If you include
driver syntax, the copyable code reflects [project](atlas-ui-query-bar-project), [sort by a field](atlas-ui-query-bar-sort),
[maxTimeMS](atlas-ui-query-bar-max-time-ms), [collation](atlas-ui-query-bar-collation), [skip](atlas-ui-query-bar-skip), and
[limit](atlas-ui-query-bar-limit) options.

sections.heading: Step 5: Click the copy  icon.
sections.body:
Click the copy icon at the top-right corner of the formatted
query to copy the query for the selected language to your clipboard. You
can now integrate and execute your created query in your application.
